mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. It provides a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool, which is necessary for managing Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure you have Python 3.10-3.13 installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify the nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that all nodes in the cluster are ready and operational.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Deploy Langflow in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for Langflow.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace langflow

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This command removes Langflow from your cluster and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This removes the Langflow deployment.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region us-central1

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, backup your data and ensure all pods are drained. This process includes upgrading the Helm chart and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Drain the node to safely upgrade.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to network issues or cluster resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check your cluster status and ensure sufficient resources are available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image could not be found.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens when the application inside the pod fails to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available.

**Fix:**
Consider scaling up your cluster or optimizing resource requests.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Python versions below 3.10.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.7.0, ensure to update your Python version and check for deprecated features.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in the 'Running' state when everything is working.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
